messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "framework-agreements-close-first-buyer",
 "title": "The declared 'large operators' beachhead is pre-signed: global framework agreements close the 40% buyer cell before the first sales call",
 "narrative": "The venture's own profile names large operators as the first buyer, and the ledger prices that cell at 0.40 of PDU spend with a comfortable 0.33–0.47 band. But the band models uncertainty about SIZE, not AVAILABILITY. The procurement record shows large operators buying rack power through multi-year global framework agreements and all-in integrated contracts (the Digital Realty–Schneider $373M framework pattern; Iren's all-in Dell deal; Jülich bundling PDU into a single IT line item). A framework agreement is not a competitor to out-sell — it is a contract that removes the tender from the market for its whole term. If a majority of the 0.40 cell is framework-governed, the entrant's declared beachhead is contractually closed for exactly its Year-1 window, and the YAM is silently re-based onto the slower mid-market and channel cells.",
 "category": "competitive",
 "targetNodes": [
  "cust.operator-large",
  "obtainableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "CE large operators renew or extend multi-year global rack-power framework agreements with Schneider/Vertiv/Eaton — the same vendors whose named major customers (Equinix, Telefonica, Vodafone) are the CE operator base",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cust-mix#c12",
    "digest:cr3-vendors#c1",
    "ledger:cust.operator-large"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "PDU line items ride the framework or the integrated OEM package; no standalone, contestable RFQ ever reaches the market for that operator's builds during the term — the entrant's pre-launch, first-12-months window (profile: Stage) coincides with mid-term, not renewal, for most frameworks",
   "refs": [
    "profile:Stage",
    "ledger:cust.operator-large",
    "digest:cust-mix#c0"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The addressable fraction of cust.operator-large collapses from 0.40 to roughly the framework-gap residual (~0.20); because the ledger's four channel values are unsourced internal estimates, nothing in the model currently resists this collapse",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:cust.operator-large",
    "ledger:obtainableFactor",
    "digest:cust-mix#c0"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "'Large operators buy direct' reads as good news on the founder's risk list — direct means no channel margin and a nameable account list. The surface appearance of reachability (you know exactly who to call) hides the contract structure: direct purchasing at that scale is framework purchasing, and the sensitivity band on the node models estimation error, not contractual foreclosure.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "Two or more CE large operators (Equinix/NTT/Data4-class) issuing standalone, multi-vendor rack-PDU RFQs open to non-framework vendors within the next 12 months.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.5,
  "rationale": "Multiple documented procurement artifacts (Digital Realty–Schneider framework, Iren all-in contract, Jülich bundled line item) all point the same direction; what is uncertain is the fraction of CE operator spend covered, not the mechanism's existence.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "cust.operator-large",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.2,
   "note": "Framework-governed spend removed from the addressable large-operator cell; residual is framework gaps, expiries and carve-outs"
  },
  {
   "nodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "op": "scale",
   "value": 0.7,
   "note": "Year-1 pace slows further because the remaining open demand sits in channels the venture did not plan to enter first"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Global or EMEA rack-power framework renewals announced by Schneider/Vertiv/Eaton with named CE operators",
   "where": "Schneider and Vertiv press rooms; DataCenterDynamics procurement coverage; operator annual reports (supplier commitments notes)",
   "threshold": "Any CE major renewing a ≥3-year rack-power framework during the venture's launch year",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Standalone rack-PDU lots appearing in European public/colo tenders",
   "where": "TED (tenders.europa.eu) CPV codes for power distribution equipment; national procurement portals (DE: bund.de vergabe)",
   "threshold": "Fewer than 2 standalone, multi-vendor PDU lots per quarter across CE-7",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Structured interviews with 3 CE operator procurement leads specifically on framework coverage, term dates, and second-source carve-out clauses",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "cust.operator-large",
   "note": "Replaces the unsourced 0.40 with a framework-adjusted addressable share and yields an expiry calendar"
  },
  {
   "action": "Build the Year-1 account plan around framework expiry dates and approved-second-source slots rather than open-market wins",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Frameworks typically permit qualified alternates; entering as second source converts foreclosure into a wedge"
  },
  {
   "action": "Negotiate pilot clauses with operators' innovation/energy teams that sit outside procurement frameworks (retrofit metering pilots)",
   "type": "contractual",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Pilot spend often bypasses framework governance thresholds"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "competitive-foreclosure",
 "riskId": "risk.competitive-foreclosure.framework-agreements-close-first-buyer"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).
